name: @workspace/imported-app

scripts.start: DATABASE_URL=file:../db/custom.db NODE_ENV=production HOST=0.0.0.0 PORT=${PORT:-3000} node server.mjs
scripts.electron:dev: pnpm run electron:build && cross-env NEXT_DEV_URL=http://localhost:3000 electron dist-electron/main.js
scripts.package: cross-env NEXT_OUTPUT_STANDALONE=1 pnpm run build && pnpm run electron:build && pnpm run strip:workspace-symlinks && electron-builder --config electron-builder.yml && pnpm run downloads:manifest
scripts.package:win: cross-env NEXT_OUTPUT_STANDALONE=1 pnpm run build && pnpm run electron:build && pnpm run strip:workspace-symlinks && electron-builder --config electron-builder.yml --win --publish never && pnpm run downloads:manifest
scripts.package:mac: cross-env NEXT_OUTPUT_STANDALONE=1 pnpm run build && pnpm run electron:build && pnpm run strip:workspace-symlinks && electron-builder --config electron-builder.yml --mac && pnpm run downloads:manifest

_ndi_note: NDI output is implemented via koffi (FFI) calling directly into Processing.NDI.Lib.x64.dll from the user's NDI Tools/Runtime install. koffi ships a precompiled win32-x64 .node binary inside its npm tarball, so there is NO node-gyp / Visual Studio Build Tools / electron-rebuild step. The runtime DLL is located at app start by walking NDI_RUNTIME_DIR_V6 / V5 / V4 env vars and the standard NDI install path. See electron/ndi-service.ts.
_dep_trim_note: v0.7.35 deep dep trim for Cloud Run cr-2-4 (4 GB) build OOM. Removed 17 fully-unused deps confirmed via `rg` to have ZERO importers anywhere in src/, app/, electron/, or root configs (only references were package.json + bun.lock): @dnd-kit/{core,sortable,utilities}, @hookform/resolvers, @reactuses/core, @tanstack/react-{query,table}, cmdk, date-fns, embla-carousel-react, input-otp, next-auth, next-intl, react-day-picker, react-hook-form, vaul, yjs. Also deleted 6 dead shadcn UI wrappers with 0 importers: src/components/ui/{carousel,drawer,calendar,input-otp,command,form}.tsx. NOTE: `ws` was initially in the removal list but is required by server-transcribe-stream.mjs (the Deepgram WebSocket proxy attached to the standalone Next prod server) — kept it. Combined with Task #92's earlier 120 MB trim, this slashes webpack's module-graph working set so the build can fit in 4 GB without bumping the build runner. next-auth alone pulled jose+oauth4webapi+openid-client+preact (huge tree). If a future feature genuinely needs any of these, re-add them with `pnpm --filter @workspace/imported-app add <pkg>`.
